body:
Our huge deadline is just hours away. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTY5NDg5NQS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMTEyOTM2MQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
| Dear Friend, I'll cut to the chase. We're closing in on our fundraising goal -- but we still need **$5,385** to hit our target. So this is the last time we'll ask before the most critical deadline of our campaign: With midnight just hours away, we need to pick up the pace or we won't make it. And that would be a huge blow to our field operations as we work to get out the vote and fight back against Republican Bill Bryant's attacks. [**Will you rush a donation to make sure we make our goal? Peter,

Would you invest $20.17 to build a safer, stronger and more prosperous America?

It may seem like a strange question, I know. _But here 's what I'm getting at._

**If every one of my father 's supporters made a one-time contribution of just $20.17, Hillary Clinton's big-dollar fundraising advantage would be completely wiped away.** We'd absolutely blow her numbers out of the water.

And why $20.17?

**Because that 's the year we're going to change our nation's course, when my father takes the oath of office and gets to work to Make America Great Again.**

So I'm making a list, and I'm going to share it with my father tomorrow.

It will include the name of every supporter who joins today to help us finish strong and WIN in 23 days. I'd love to add your name to the list.

[**Please JOIN Team Trump today with a $20.17 special contribution - or more if you can - by following this secure link.**](http://email.granitelists.com/ct/38147814:WTmvPRbUN:m:1:1155937413:FD7AD50C323FF14EBA475C4B74B62E53:r)

**But Crooked Hillary Clinton continues to raise huge sums from Wall Street banks and special interests, leftist elites like George Soros and Hollywood liberals.** This is allowing her to outspend us big time on the airwaves.

[**Make a statement today - help us close the gap, by joining my father in this fight with a $20.17 special contribution.**](http://email.granitelists.com/ct/38147814:WTmvPRbUN:m:1:1155937413:FD7AD50C323FF14EBA475C4B74B62E53:r)

Thank you - I hope you choose to get involved.

Sincerely,

Eric Trump

subject: First 100 days
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Make America Great Again Committee, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Donald J
body:
We're going to do big things to turn our country around. Here's my agenda for the first 100 days of a Trump White House. |
[](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecjbaT-xBADC1cBSfOoaGDWrDRMN6aaaaGDWrBMAHKYfa?l=~@t9rBr-&V=mg5ZucrjDy_pdjZu.Txd&u=T&6=&i=-r-x-xHfwVHY3eminUHUjp112gp1sWl12121qWHV&212)

Friend,

First and foremost I want to thank you.

With your help we've built an amazing movement with the best supporters, and we're going to dominate these last few weeks of the campaign and win.

But we can't leave anything to chance. [**Your continued support is CRUCIAL - make a $250, $150, $100, $75, $50 or $25 contribution today to help us finish strong.**](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecjbaT-xBADC1cBSfOoaGDWrDRMN6aaaaGDWrBMAHKYfa?l=~@t9rBr-&V=mg5ZucrjDy_pdjZu.Txd&u=U&6=&i=-r-x-xHfwVHY3eminUHUjp112gp1sWl12121qWHV&212)

I'm ready to get to work for the American people on January 20, 2017, and I know we will accomplish really big things to turn our country around.

**Here 's what I'll do during my first 100 days in office:**
* Appoint judges who will uphold our Constitution - this is so important;

* Stand up to the many, many countries that are ripping us off on trade;

* Change federal immigration rules to give our people the opportunity to fill good-paying jobs, and cancel regulations that send our jobs overseas;

* Lift the job-killing Obama restrictions on energy production;

* Repeal and replace Obamacare - it's been an absolute DISASTER for families.

Your support has been a tremendous asset to me in this race... and with less than four weeks to go we can't let up for a moment.

There are two things I'm asking you do to today:

1) Forward this email to your family and friends, so they know exactly what's going to happen in the first 100 days of a Trump White House to Make America Great Again;

2) [**Make a special contribution by following this link to help us close out the final weeks of this campaign strong and WIN.**](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecjbaT-xBADC1cBSfOoaGDWrDRMN6aaaaGDWrBMAHKYfa?l=~@t9rBr-&V=mg5ZucrjDy_pdjZu.Txd&u=V&6=&i=-r-x-xHfwVHY3eminUHUjp112gp1sWl12121qWHV&212)

Best wishes,

[](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecjbaT-xBADC1cBSfOoaGDWrDRMN6aaaaGDWrBMAHKYfa?l=~@t9rBr-&V=mg5ZucrjDy_pdjZu.Txd&u=W&6=&i=-r-x-xHfwVHY3eminUHUjp112gp1sWl12121qWHV&212)

Donald J. Trump

**[CONTRIBUTE $100](http://rnctrack.hosted.strongview.com/t/ecjbaT-xBADC1cBSfOoaGDWrDRMN6aaaaGDWrBMAHKYfa?l=~@t9rBr-&V=mg5ZucrjDy_pdjZu.Txd&u=S&6=&i=-r-x-xHfwVHY3eminUHUjp112gp1sWl12121qWHV&212)**

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: from the grassroots up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Friend, I first met Chris discussing our different musical tastes, favorite movies and yes -- debating nuclear weapons policy and the nuclear freeze movement. Little did I know that 30 years later his passion to make our communities and our world a safer and better place would still be a defining theme of our lives together.

But I should have guessed, because Chris has always been a strong believer that government can be an important force for good. Sadly, we are living in a time when the radical ideology of groups like the Tea Party are undermining faith in our democracy. For the last 6 years Republican control of the Congress has enabled these extremists to control the agenda and block efforts to make progress.

That's why Washington Republicans and their big-money friends are determined not to lose their majority. They're trying to turn the tide of this election in races across the country--and Maryland won't be an exception.

Over the past few months, top Republicans like Scott Walker and Carly Fiorina have been helping fund our Republican opponent's agenda. They -- just like the NRA and a shady SuperPAC that has already started attacking Chris -- know that our opponent will stand with them if she's elected.

Chris won't.

Chris has spent his time in public service fighting to keep dark money out of politics and end gun violence, and advancing the issues that matter most to the people of Maryland. And when he's elected to the U.S. Senate, his opponent's big-money friends know that he'll keep fighting for our values and priorities.

Early voting begins in Maryland in less than 30 days, friend. Will you chip in $ or whatever you can right now to help keep us reach our September goal before Friday's FEC deadline?

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Donate $5 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $10 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $25 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $50 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $100 to stand with Chris >>>

Donate $200 to stand with Chris >>>

Or donate another amount to stand with Chris >>>

Polls show that 19% of the electorate in Maryland is still undecided. We need to do better than that. With less than one month until early voting we must do everything we can to turn out as many voters as we possible and move our state and our country forward.

Thank you for being part of this,

Katherine
Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate

name: Eric Hyers
subject: I wanted to loop you in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P
body:
---
Friend,

With just three weeks to go until Election Day, I wanted to take time to give you a state-of-the-race update. It's a little long but as we ask for your continued support, I wanted to loop you in to some of the inner workings of our campaign right now.

Please take a minute to read. **Voting has begun and we 're depending on our committed grassroots supporters to make sure we're fully funded during the homestretch. **[Kick in $18.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=qtUQrMDMS26hb1gj4%2ByUkzCDQeO7ebtR)**

**_Here's where we're at:_**

**Lee Newspapers is out with a new poll that has Steve up with a narrow two-point lead. **This is right in line with what we expected down the stretch -- a close contest in which Steve has the edge.

**Greg Gianforte has once again upped his television ad spending, adding $256,000 for the next week. _**He's now poured in $3.3 million of his fortune and has outspent us by more than 4:1 on the airwaves.**_

Four attack commercials against Steve are currently airing on TV, Gianforte has radio spots on across the state and a mountain of fear-mongering mail is being sent to Montanans.

_**How we 're fighting back:**_

**_So far Steve has outraised Greg Gianforte, not counting for his self-funding._** That's actual grassroots supporters who are pitching in their hard-earned dollars to make sure Steve is reelected.

We're running an issue-based campaign with none of those fear-mongering, grainy attack ads you'll keep seeing from the Gianforte campaign. We _**will**_ continue to highlight the clear contrast on fiscal responsibility, public education, non-discrimination, women's rights, fair tax policy and access to public lands.

Working with the Democratic coordinated campaign we are running a top-notch, data-driven ground game that has spent the last several months identifying supporters and will work the next three weeks to ensure that we get out the vote. There are almost fifty field staffers working out of ten offices -- an operation like this can give our campaign a 3-5 point advantage when final votes are tallied.

The thing is, all of this work to counteract Greg Gianforte's millions isn't cheap. **[We're proud of the operation we're running and we hope that you'll support our efforts with a contribution of $18.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=dsxBP34NcgVPAkezHQy7d%2B3KVXbEsJYq)**

Thanks,

Eric Hyers
Campaign Manager ********
---

If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=94OoI8ldR0mAIVNA%2B687mjCDQeO7ebtR).

name: Jane Kleeb, OurRevolution.com
subject: 45 years in prison for filming a pipeline protest?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
A filmmaker journalist was arrested for filming a pipeline protest in North Dakota. Deia Schlosberg was held in jail for 48 hours without access to her attorney, and now she's facing felony conspiracy charges. **We need to get the government to drop these charges, which are an affront to the First Amendment.**
[**Sign our petition to the U.S. Attorney to drop the charges against journalist Deia Schlosberg.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full")
**[ADD YOUR NAME»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full")**
Sisters and Brothers,
Native American tribal nations, farmers, and activists are protesting the Dakota Access pipeline for fracked oil, which would bring some of the dirtiest oil on the planet across five states.
Journalists and filmmakers have been at pipeline protests throughout North Dakota, documenting the bravery of activists who have been pepper sprayed and attacked with dogs for trying to protect native land and water sources.
**But this week took a turn for the worse: a filmmaker at another solidarity protest in North Dakota against Tar Sands oil was arrested and charged with conspiracy for filming an action by activists to stop five pipelines from operating.** Deia Schlosberg had her footage confiscated before being held in jail for two days and was not allowed to speak to an attorney. Now she's facing felony charges of conspiracy for filming the protest, and if Deia is convicted, she could face up to 45 years in prison.
We need to pressure the government to drop charges against Deia Schlosberg. **Our best chance is to ask U.S. Attorney for North Dakota, Chris Myers, to drop the charges** , and to make sure North Dakota Governor Jack Dalrymple and President Obama's Department of Justice also know Deia should not be charged.
[**Sign our petition for the government to drop all charges against Deia Schlosberg, a filmmaker and journalist who was arrested just for filming a protest. Add your name here.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full")
Arresting journalists for documenting environmental actions is a dangerous precedent and an affront to the First Amendment.
Deia Schlosberg is an experienced and widely acclaimed filmmaker and journalist. She was the producer for Our Revolution Climate advisor Josh Fox's latest climate change documentary, _How to Let Go of The World and Love All The Things Climate Can 't Change_, and her work documenting environmental organizing is respected and critical.
These charges are a threat to our freedom of expression on the most basic level and especially to documentary filmmaking. If filmmakers cannot film events as they're happening, especially protest events or events that the establishment and the police might consider criminal, our society will not be the same.
Pipelines that transport oil are some of the biggest threats to climate change. Pipelines can carry millions of gallons of dirty oil a day, and activists are doing all they can to stop these pipelines from being built or from operating.
People like Deia Schlosberg risk their safety to show these protests to the world, but now she's facing up to 45 years in prison for telling this story about threats to our climate.
[**Sign the petition to U.S. Attorney Chris Myers, North Dakota Governor Jack Dalrymple, and the Department of Justice to drop charges against Deia Schlosberg for filming a pipeline protest.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/pipeline-charges?source=em161015-full")
Thank you for all you do to help stop the effects of climate change.
In solidarity,

clean_body: A filmmaker journalist was arrested for filming a pipeline protest in North Dakota. Deia Schlosberg was held in jail for 48 hours without access to her attorney, and now she's facing felony conspiracy charges. We need to get the government to drop these charges, which are an affront to the First Amendment. "" "" Sisters and Brothers, Native American tribal nations, farmers, and activists are protesting the Dakota Access pipeline for fracked oil, which would bring some of the dirtiest oil on the planet across five states. Journalists and filmmakers have been at pipeline protests throughout North Dakota, documenting the bravery of activists who have been pepper sprayed and attacked with dogs for trying to protect native land and water sources. But this week took a turn for the worse: a filmmaker at another solidarity protest in North Dakota against Tar Sands oil was arrested and charged with conspiracy for filming an action by activists to stop five pipelines from operating. Deia Schlosberg had her footage confiscated before being held in jail for two days and was not allowed to speak to an attorney. Now she's facing felony charges of conspiracy for filming the protest, and if Deia is convicted, she could face up to 45 years in prison. We need to pressure the government to drop charges against Deia Schlosberg. Our best chance is to ask U.S. Attorney for North Dakota, Chris Myers, to drop the charges , and to make sure North Dakota Governor Jack Dalrymple and President Obama's Department of Justice also know Deia should not be charged. "" Arresting journalists for documenting environmental actions is a dangerous precedent and an affront to the First Amendment. Deia Schlosberg is an experienced and widely acclaimed filmmaker and journalist. She was the producer for Our Revolution Climate advisor Josh Fox's latest climate change documentary, How to Let Go of The World and Love All The Things Climate Can 't Change, and her work documenting environmental organizing is respected and critical. These charges are a threat to our freedom of expression on the most basic level and especially to documentary filmmaking. If filmmakers cannot film events as they're happening, especially protest events or events that the establishment and the police might consider criminal, our society will not be the same. Pipelines that transport oil are some of the biggest threats to climate change. Pipelines can carry millions of gallons of dirty oil a day, and activists are doing all they can to stop these pipelines from being built or from operating. People like Deia Schlosberg risk their safety to show these protests to the world, but now she's facing up to 45 years in prison for telling this story about threats to our climate.
